message: Great, then you already know how challenges with executive functioning, such as organization and planning can have on daily functioning.
Is ADHD something I can cure or get rid of?

ADHD persists into adulthood, but some people may experience a reduction in symptoms, like hyperactivity, as they age. Strategies and medication, such as stimulants, can help regulate attention and impulse control.

Actually, there are a lot of people out there with ADHD.  It is one of the most common disabilities of childhood. You are not alone.  About 5 out of every 100 students your age have ADHD. In a regular classroom of 30 students, there will be 1-2 students with ADHD.
That is a lot of people.
It is. Chances are you know at least a couple of people with ADHD.
